key_points: Rotational symmetry occurs when a figure fits onto itself during a rotation
The center of rotation is the fixed point around which the object turns
Order of rotational symmetry is the number of times it looks the same in $360^{\circ}$
Angle of rotation is the minimum angle required to reach a symmetric position
examples: Order 3: Equilateral triangle, Ceiling fan with 3 blades
formulas: Order of symmetry for regular polygon $\rightarrow n$ (number of sides)
exam_tips: Every object has rotational symmetry of order 1 ($360^{\circ}$), so we only count if order $> 1$
To find the center of rotation, imagine pinning the shape at a point and spinning it
A parallelogram has rotational symmetry but NO line symmetry
Clockwise and anti-clockwise rotations result in the same order
